stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags including 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates ongoing community interest and benchmarking activity around a significant open-source LLM.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML topics including transformers, safetensors, and text-generation.
The mention of Meta's Llama series indicates relevance within the competitive large language model space.

stories.title: OpenAI unveils GPT-5.6 amid US AI regulatory drama
stories.author: Hayden Field
bullet_points: OpenAI has released a new model suite, GPT-5.6, featuring three tiers: Sol, Terra, and Luna.
The release follows recent reports regarding regulatory influence from the Trump administration on OpenAI's release schedule.
The new suite offers differentiated models for various use cases, including a flagship model (Sol) and a high-volume option (Terra).

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation tasks, indicating its capability in generative AI.
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors, placing it within the current LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the DeepSeek-V4-Pro model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI pipelines.
It utilizes standard ML components such as transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon.
The performance boost is attributed to updates in the MLX engine.
Improvements include faster response times, higher output quality, and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance enhancement.
This update specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing LLM inference on Apple hardware.
The feature is currently available in preview, signaling a push for hardware-specific AI acceleration.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed for local execution.
It integrates messaging apps with local AI coding agents.
The core value proposition is running AI agents entirely on the user's own device for privacy/control.

stories.title: Notion killing Skiff-influenced email app since most users use AI agents instead
stories.author: Scharon Harding
bullet_points: Notion is heavily integrating AI agents to manage user inboxes.
This move suggests a shift where AI agents are replacing traditional email application functionality.
The development targets users who might otherwise use specialized email clients like Skiff.

stories.title: When Does Combining Language Models Help? A Co-Failure Ceiling on Routing, Voting, and Mixture-of-Agents Across 67 Frontier Models
stories.author: Josef Chen
bullet_points: The paper analyzes the performance limits of multi-model LLM systems (routing, voting, MoA) designed to surpass single-model accuracy.
It establishes a theoretical 'Co-Failure Ceiling,' showing that accuracy is fundamentally capped by the rate ($\beta$) at which all constituent models fail on the same query.
The study highlights a limitation in current diagnostic metrics, noting that standard error correlation ($\rho$) cannot accurately identify this crucial failure rate ($\beta$).

stories.title: Prompt Injection in Automated Résumé Screening with Large Language Models: Single and Multi-Injection Settings
stories.author: Stefanus Jasin
bullet_points: The research investigates 'prompt injection' specifically within LLM-powered résumé screening systems.
Prompt injection is defined as subtle, self-promotional text designed to manipulate LLM evaluations without adding new qualifications.
Controlled experiments demonstrate that this injection technique reliably improves applicant rankings when résumé quality is otherwise similar.

stories.title: Reinforcement Learning without Ground-Truth Solutions can Improve LLMs
stories.author: Yuxiong He
bullet_points: Introduces RiVER, a framework for Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) that bypasses the need for ground-truth solutions when training LLMs.
RiVER trains LLMs using score-based optimization tasks, leveraging deterministic execution feedback as continuous-valued supervision.
This advancement significantly broadens the applicability of RL techniques to complex, real-world tasks where definitive correct answers are unknown.

stories.title: Hallucination in World Models is Predictable and Preventable
stories.author: Xiaolong Wang
bullet_points: The paper addresses the critical issue of 'hallucination' in generative world models, where simulated futures drift from reality.
The authors hypothesize that hallucinations occur in sparsely covered regions of the state-action space, suggesting a data-centric solution.
They introduce MMBench2, a large-scale dataset designed to test and guide mitigation strategies for visual world modeling.

stories.title: Previewing GPT‑5.6 Sol: a next-generation model
stories.author: minimaxir
bullet_points: The article previews GPT-5.6 Sol, indicating a next-generation model from OpenAI.
The release is accessible via a specific deployment safety link provided by OpenAI.
This signals ongoing, significant advancements in large language model development.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving support on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating the use of local LLMs.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for running coding tools locally or in the cloud.
It supports various coding models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now offers experimental local image generation capabilities on macOS.
This feature allows users to run image generation models entirely on their local machines.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Antibiotic "megacluster" discovery provides new strategy to fight superbugs
stories.author: Beth Mole
bullet_points: A new antibiotic 'megacluster' has been discovered.
This discovery offers a novel strategy in the fight against antibiotic-resistant superbugs.
The finding is considered an important step in replenishing the global antibiotic supply.

stories.title: VW may close four factories to adapt to the future, report says
stories.author: Jonathan M. Gitlin
bullet_points: Volkswagen Group is planning a significant restructuring of its operations.
The restructuring is being driven by declining sales, particularly in the US and China markets.
The plan includes the potential closure of four VW factories to adapt to future market conditions.

stories.title: SpaceX plans to launch Starlink mobile service in the US
stories.author: Kieran Smith, George Steer, James Fontanella-Khan, and Michelle Chan, Financial Times
bullet_points: SpaceX is planning to launch a Starlink mobile service within the US.
This move represents a significant test for SpaceX's ability to transition from satellite infrastructure to a mass-market consumer phone business.
The launch aims to validate the commercial viability of their satellite-based mobile connectivity.

stories.title: FCC may kill $2B program that connects schools and libraries to Internet
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: The FCC is reportedly considering terminating a $2 billion program designed to provide internet access to schools and libraries.
The potential cancellation is linked to concerns raised by Carr regarding student screen time.
The criticism suggests Carr's involvement is perceived as overreach, attempting to act as a 'nation's parent'.

stories.title: Language-Based Digital Twins for Elderly Cognitive Assistance
stories.author: Hiroko H. Dodge
bullet_points: Proposes a language-based digital twin framework for elderly cognitive assistance.
Leverages Large Language Models (LLMs) to mimic the conversational behavior of seniors.
Aims to use conversational patterns as non-invasive biomarkers for early detection of Mild Cognitive Impairment (MCI).

stories.title: Empowering GUI Agents via Autonomous Experience Exploration and Hindsight Experience Utilization for Task Planning
stories.author: Jun Zhao
bullet_points: The research addresses the weak planning and limited generalization of small, cost-efficient Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) used for GUI agent tasks.
A new method, Planning Experience Exploration and Utilization (PEEU), is introduced to improve autonomous task planning for web agents.
The work focuses on enabling GUI agents to handle complex, repetitive tasks by autonomously exploring environments and utilizing past experiences.

stories.title: AI Healthcare Chatbots as Information Infrastructure: A Large-Scale Study of User-Reported Breakdowns
stories.author: Masooda Bashir
bullet_points: The study analyzes over 15,000 user reviews across 59 AI healthcare chatbots to assess real-world performance.
Three major recurring breakdowns were identified: access barriers/service unreliability, and user experience/interaction quality.
The research highlights the need for further study on the impact of AI chatbots in health information seeking and self-management.

stories.title: DanceOPD: On-Policy Generative Field Distillation
stories.author: Tat-Seng Chua
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of unifying diverse capabilities (T2I, local/global editing) in modern image generation models.
Identifies a core problem where editing capabilities often degrade text-to-image performance or conflict with each other.
Introduces 'DanceOPD' as a new method designed to effectively compose and align these conflicting image generation capabilities.

stories.title: When are likely answers right? On Sequence Probability and Correctness in LLMs
stories.author: Jonas Geiping
bullet_points: The paper investigates the fundamental relationship between sequence probability and correctness in Large Language Models (LLMs).
It analyzes how various LLM decoding methods shift probability mass toward likely outputs, questioning when this likelihood equates to factual accuracy.
The research aims to quantify this alignment across different decoding strategies and models.

stories.title: Error-Conditioned Neural Solvers
stories.author: Jeong Joon Park
bullet_points: Neural surrogate models for solving PDEs are fast but lack inherent mechanisms to correct constraint violations or extrapolate reliably.
Existing hybrid methods improve physical correctness by minimizing the PDE residual but suffer from the computational cost and instability of classical optimizers.
The research introduces a new approach (Error-Conditioned Neural Solvers) to address the limitations of current neural PDE solvers.

stories.title: Empowering GUI Agents via Autonomous Experience Exploration and Hindsight Experience Utilization for Task Planning
stories.author: Jun Zhao
bullet_points: The research addresses the weak planning and limited generalization of small, cost-efficient Multimodal LLMs (MLLMs) used for GUI agent tasks.
A new method, Planning Experience Exploration and Utilization (PEEU), is introduced to enhance autonomous task planning in web agents.
The work focuses on enabling small, privacy-preserving MLLMs to effectively decompose and execute complex, repetitive GUI tasks.

stories.title: Empowering GUI Agents via Autonomous Experience Exploration and Hindsight Experience Utilization for Task Planning
stories.author: Jun Zhao
bullet_points: The research addresses the weak planning and generalization issues in small, cost-efficient Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) used for GUI agent tasks.
It introduces the Planning Experience Exploration and Utilization (PEEU) method to improve autonomous task planning for web agents.
The work focuses on enabling GUI agents to assist humans with repetitive tasks by decomposing complex actions.

stories.title: LLM-Based Examination of Eligibility Criteria from Securities Prospectuses at the German Central Bank
stories.author: Christian Hänig
bullet_points: The research addresses the resource-intensive task of verifying security collateral eligibility using German Central Bank prospectuses.
It proposes using Large Language Models (LLMs) to overcome limitations of traditional NER methods (e.g., handling OCR noise and linguistic variance).
The application is highly specialized, focusing on regulatory compliance and financial document analysis.

stories.title: Beyond Surface Forms: A Comprehensive, Mechanism-Oriented Taxonomy of Indirect Linguistic Encoding for LLM-Based Coded Language Detection
stories.author: Paul Jen-Hwa Hu
bullet_points: The research addresses the problem of users employing indirect linguistic expressions (ILE) to evade moderation and surveillance on social media.
It proposes a mechanism-oriented taxonomy to categorize these ILEs (like algospeak and euphemisms) based on underlying encoding operations, rather than communicative goals.
This work is highly relevant to the development of more robust and sophisticated LLM-based coded language detection systems.

stories.title: How Surprising Is Historical Italian to Language Models? Tokenization Tax, Comprehension Tax, and a Simple Mitigation
stories.author: Maria Levchenko
bullet_points: The paper addresses the poorly understood challenge of processing historical language using Large Language Models (LLMs).
It introduces a diagnostic framework to decompose historical language difficulty into four dimensions, including tokenization cost and predictive uncertainty.
The research aims to provide methods for mitigating the performance degradation LLMs experience with older or variant language forms.

stories.title: LMs as Task-Specific Knowledge Bases: An Interpretability Analysis
stories.author: Mor Geva
bullet_points: The paper investigates the hypothesis that Large Language Models (LMs) function as task-specific knowledge bases.
It analyzes whether LMs exhibit the crucial knowledge base property of returning consistent results for the same fact across different queries.
The findings indicate that LMs encode knowledge in a manner that is specific to the task they are performing.

stories.title: Show HN: Smart model routing directly in Claude, Codex and Cursor
stories.author: adchurch
bullet_points: A new model router has been developed to intelligently direct requests to the optimal LLM (e.g., Claude, Codex) within coding agents.
The primary motivation for the tool is to manage and reduce the escalating costs associated with heavy AI usage in software development.
The solution is presented as a local implementation and is relevant to developers heavily integrating AI into their coding workflows.
